response.justification: A versatile, senior/staff-level engineer with genuine AI-native engineering exposure (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling) and credible high-throughput experience (microservices handling millions of requests/second, low-latency async work). Software-craftsmanship depth (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based) is strong. However, the primary must-have — strong Python — is entirely absent from the skills list and work history; the language stack is inverted relative to the role (excellent TypeScript, but core depth is in DLang, a niche language). Staff-level technical-strategy and stakeholder-management evidence is thin, and there is no mention of on-call/incident ownership. Worth a screening conversation to probe Python depth and strategy scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Direct AI-native engineering experience: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Demonstrated high-throughput/distributed work (microservices at millions of req/s, low-latency async I/O)
Strong engineering discipline and TypeScript depth (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Python — a core must-have — is absent from skills and entire work history
Core technical depth is in DLang, a niche language with limited relevance to the role
Little evidence of staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder management, or on-call/incident ownership
